description:
Run dREG on the GPU machines. Three inputs: first two: pl and mn bigwig files; the third, pretrained model. Modified from BioHPC's website:
1. Load the docker image
docker1 pull biohpc/dreg
docker1 images

2. Prepare input data files
All data files should be put in a directory under /workdir/$USER. For example: /workdir/$USER/mydata

3. Run command
#set DOCKER command (modify the part "/workdir/$USER/mydata" based on your data directory)
export DREG="docker1 run --rm --gpus all -v /workdir/$USER/mydata:/data -w /data biohpc/dreg"
#run dREG, use 16 cpu cores, and gpu unit 0
$DREG bash /dREG/run_dREG.bsh mysample_plus.bw mysample_end_minus.bw output_prefix model.rdata 16 0

4. After you are done, get the ownership of the result files (docker run as root, so output files are owned by root)
docker1 claim /workdir/$USER/mydata
name: dREG Docker

reference.dREG_MODEL: dREG model taken from Wang, Z., Chu, T., Choate, L. A., & Danko, C. G. (2019). Identification of regulatory elements from nascent transcription using dREG. Genome research, 29(2), 293–303. doi:10.1101/gr.238279.118

step.parameter: DREG="docker1 run --rm --gpus all -v /workdir/ly349/{{Job}}:/data -w /data biohpc/dreg" && $DREG bash /dREG/run_dREG.bsh pl.bw mn.bw {{JobName}} model.rdata 16 1
